title: Sources table (citation ledger)
description: Citation ledger for the canonical store. One row per CITATION — i.e. one row per distinct piece of upstream reportage that observations reference. Natural identity = (producer, title, vintage); source_id is a deterministic hash of that triple. Pivoted from v1.0's fetch-ledger shape (keyed on url + content_hash, smeared by fetch timestamps) per ADR-0032: provenance is what the citizen cites, not what the pipeline polled. Fetch telemetry (last-polled, content_hash) moves out of the canonical contract entirely — adapters that care for cache-invalidation purposes write sidecars under .runtime/<adapter>/<source_id>.json. The table is OWID-aligned (producer, title, vintage, license, url_main, citation_full verbatim from OWID origin.*) plus yen-gov extensions (source_id PK, confidence_tier, is_issuing_authority, verification_method, notes). Authored shape is JSON (PR-reviewable, D18); pipeline compiles to taxonomy/sources.parquet for DuckDB consumption.

x-changelog.description: Initial canonical sources table schema (Phase 0.3 of TODO/20260517-canonical-long-format-pivot.md). OWID origin.* fields verbatim (producer, citation_full, url_main, url_download, date_accessed, license, vintage). yen-gov extensions tagged in property descriptions: source_id (PK), content_hash, first_fetched_at, last_seen_at, confidence_tier, is_issuing_authority. Per D5/D6.

ADR-0032 P.0e: pivot from fetch-ledger to citation-ledger. Natural identity moves from (url, content_hash) to (producer, title, vintage). Removed (breaking): url, content_hash, url_download, date_accessed, first_fetched_at, last_seen_at — all fetch telemetry leaves the canonical contract; fetch-cache adapters write sidecars under .runtime/<adapter>/<source_id>.json. Added (breaking required): title, vintage, verification_method (live-fetch / archived-snapshot / transcribed / editorial). vintage was nullable optional in v1.0; now required (citation identity depends on it; empty string permitted when the source publishes no vintage). source_id pattern tightened from ^src-[a-z0-9]{8,}$ to ^src-[a-z0-9]{12}$ (12 hex chars from sha256 of the citation triple, deterministic per backend/yen_gov/canonical/citation.derive_source_id). url_main + citation_full + notes are optional; license is enum-locked. See ADR-0032 + docs/concepts/data-provenance.md.

source_id.description: yen-gov extension (not OWID). PK; deterministic 12-char prefix of sha256(f'{producer}|{title}|{vintage}'). NOT random and NOT minted by an autoincrement — the same citation triple anywhere in the codebase yields the same source_id. Built by backend/yen_gov/canonical/citation.derive_source_id().

producer.description: OWID origin.producer verbatim. Publisher organisation (e.g. 'Election Commission of India', 'Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation', 'Reserve Bank of India'). For hand-authored: 'yen-gov'. Part of the citation identity triple.

title.description: OWID origin.title verbatim. The thing being cited (e.g. 'Statistical Report Section 10 (Detailed Results) — TN', 'National Family Health Survey, India Report'). Part of the citation identity triple. Citizen-facing; include enough discriminator (state / report family / volume) that two distinct citations don't collide on title alone.

vintage.description: OWID origin.vintage verbatim — source's OWN period / revision / edition label (e.g. 'AcGenApr2021', 'FY 2024-25', '2025 release', 'Provisional Estimates', 'NFHS-5'). Preserved as-published; never normalised. Required string; permitted to be empty when the source genuinely publishes no vintage (rare). Part of the citation identity triple — distinct vintages of the same producer+title get distinct source_ids.

license.description: OWID origin.license verbatim — locked enum. 'OGL-IN-1.0' = National Data Sharing & Accessibility Policy (most Indian government open data). 'CC-BY-4.0' / 'CC0-1.0' = explicit CC license declared by publisher. 'public-domain' = explicit public-domain dedication. 'unknown-public' = published publicly but no explicit license — cite under fair-use defaults. 'internal' = yen-gov hand-authored content.

confidence_tier.description: yen-gov extension (not OWID). gold = issuing authority publishing its own primary data (ECI, MoSPI, RBI). silver = reputable secondary republisher / research aggregator (PRS, CMIE, OWID-imported). bronze = single-paper / unverified mirror — surface with citizen-visible caveat. Curation discipline for Indian mixed-quality data shelves; OWID's editorial gate happens upstream of the table so they don't carry an equivalent.

is_issuing_authority.description: yen-gov extension (not OWID). True iff producer is the official issuing authority for this data (e.g. ECI for election results, NCRB for crime). False for republishers. Often implied by confidence_tier=gold but explicit because the rare gold-republisher case (a research aggregator we trust for accuracy without being the issuer) needs to read True for confidence and False for citation-authority.

verification_method.description: yen-gov extension (not OWID). How yen-gov knows the citation reflects what the producer actually published. 'live-fetch' = adapter calls upstream HTTP each ingest run and operates on the response bytes directly. 'archived-snapshot' = adapter operates on a local copy of bytes that were fetched in a prior session (most ECI Statistical Reports — XLSX held locally; source URL no longer always live). 'transcribed' = human read the source and typed values in (rare; used only when no machine-readable form exists). 'editorial' = yen-gov hand-authored content (license='internal'). Trust ordering live-fetch > archived-snapshot > transcribed > editorial — see backend/yen_gov/canonical/citation.verification_method_rank().

url_main.description: OWID origin.url_main verbatim. Landing / about / download page URL the citizen can click through to. Optional + nullable — many archived snapshots no longer have a live URL. When null the citation is still complete; the citizen sees (producer, title, vintage) plus a 'no live URL' indicator.

citation_full.description: OWID origin.citation_full verbatim. Full bibliographic citation string for academic-style attribution. Optional — when null, backend/yen_gov/canonical/citation.render_citation() composes a default from (producer, title, vintage).

notes.description: yen-gov extension. Operator-visible free-text note (e.g. 'XLSX layout changed between AcGenMay2021 and AcGenApr2024 — see adapter README'). Optional; NOT citizen-facing.
